title: Mazzic
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Character

|homeworld=[[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]<ref name="TLC-SB">''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]''</ref>

{{Quote|We're businessmen, pure and simple; and I for one intend to stay that way. If the New Republic can outbid the Empire for information, I'll be happy to sell it to them. If not—|Mazzic|<ref name="TLC">''[[The Last Command]]''</ref>}}
'''Mazzic''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] from [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. Originally a free trader, he turned to smuggling and ran an independent smuggling [[Mazzic's organization|organization]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Mazzic hired the [[Mistryl Shadow Guard]] [[Shada D'ukal]] as his bodyguard where she hid her deadly abilities and posed as his female companion.

During [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Mazzic joined [[Talon Karrde]]'s [[Smugglers' Alliance]] after his friend, [[Lishma]], was killed by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces during a meeting at [[Whistler's Whirlpool Tapcafe]] on [[Trogan/Legends|Trogan]]. He [[Raid on Bilbringi|struck]] back at the Empire, destroying a half-completed [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] being constructed at the [[Bilbringi Shipyards]].

==Biography==
===Early life===
[[File:Corellia SOF.png|thumb|left|250px|Corellia, Mazzic's homeworld]]
Mazzic was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] male [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]<ref name="TLC-SB" /> in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA-OC">{{SW|url=atlas|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|archivedate=20090911050506}}</ref> Mazzic eventually left his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], although he flatly refused to discuss the reason why with anyone. He became a [[free-trader]], running cargo and goods for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] until they double-crossed him. During the altercation, a [[Mazzic's friend|friend]] of his was [[Death/Legends|killed]]; some speculated that the dead friend was, in fact, Mazzic's [[Marriage/Legends|wife]],<ref name="TLC-SB" /> and that the Empire murdered her in an effort to get to Mazzic.<ref name="Gamer 3" /> Leaving Imperial space, Mazzic struck out for the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]], running legitimate cargo. However, he found dealing with the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] to be worse than dealing with the Empire. Mazzic soon began [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] weapons to contract laborers on Corporate Sector planets, and even extracting workers from their work camps while evading Authority [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]]. However, Mazzic actively denied events from his past, covering his tracks and cultivating a background that was cloaked in rumor and conjecture. As a result, little was actually known about Mazzic, and that was the way he liked it.<ref name="TLC-SB" />

===Expanding operations===
After discovering the [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|profits]] to be made from smuggling, Mazzic returned to Imperial [[Realspace/Legends|space]] and began running contraband where he gained a reputation for successfully evading [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]] [[Customs official/Legends|officials]].<ref name="TLC-SB" /> By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> Mazzic had been an active smuggler for several [[Standard year/Legends|years]], and had established a [[Mazzic's organization|small organization]]. Fellow smuggler [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] ran cargo for Mazzic on several occasions.<ref name="Scoundrels">''[[Scoundrels]]''</ref>

[[File:Starwind.png|thumb|right|250px|The ''Distant Rainbow'', Mazzic's ''Starwind''-class pleasure yacht]]
While building his smuggling organization, Mazzic purchased a [[Starwind-class pleasure yacht|''Starwind''-class pleasure yacht]] from a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Unidentified Rodian smuggler|smuggler]] who had decided to retire to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Naming the ship ''[[Distant Rainbow]]'', Mazzic used it as his personal [[Starship/Legends|vessel]], modifying the ship by enhancing its weapons<ref name="TLC-SB" /> to include dorsal and ventral [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannons]]. Mazzic based the design off [[Hologram/Legends|holos]] he's seen of Solo's ship, the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'', although he would never admit to it.<ref name="Gamer 4">{{GamerCite|4|Ships of the Smuggler's Alliance}}</ref> He also upgraded the countermeasures packages<ref name="TLC-SB" /> with a suite designed to fool casual [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] scans and augmented the [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] [[Engine/Legends|engine]]s<ref name="Gamer 4" /> to improve the ship's lift-to-mass ratio.<ref name="TLC-SB" />

The pride of Mazzic's fleet, however, were the two [[Preybird-class starfighter|''Preybird''-class starfighter]]s, ''[[Raptor (Preybird)|Raptor]]'' and ''[[Skyclaw]]''. Mazzic had been able to observe the protoype of the fighters around [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] through connections at [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] when the company were attempting to market the fighters and became enamoured with the design. Due to budget considerations, the Preybird line was discontinued, with only a few production models made. Mazzic acquired two of the ships at a reduced price through his contact. Over the course of several years, Mazzic overhauled the [[Flight computer/Legends|flight computers]] and weapon systems on the ships to create deadly war machines.<ref name="TLC-SB" /> Intimidating to behold, more than one better-armed ship surrendered to Mazzic when faced with the pair of Preybirds. Mazzic's main concern was that the vessels would one day be destroyed as he knew replacing them would be an expensive proposition.<ref name="Gamer 4" />

[[File:WinterScoundrels.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Mazzic's operative, Winter]]
In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Mazzic recruited a young woman named [[Winter Celchu|Winter]] into his organization. She was a specialist in procurement operations, tracking shipping manifests and reviewing warehouse records to find information and equipment Mazzic needed. The information usually consisted of individuals attempting to hide their merchandise, so Mazzic contacted them and offered his smuggling services. The equipment was simply stolen if Mazzic wanted it. Two [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after recruiting Winter, Mazzic employed [[Kell Tainer]], a young Human demolitions expert.<ref name="Scoundrels">''[[Scoundrels]]''</ref>

Winter was also an [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|intelligence]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], and had been assigned to work undercover in Mazzic's organization. She used his resources to locate supply and weapons storehouses, then after Mazzic's people had removed whatever they needed, she arranged for the Alliance to sweep in behind them and take the rest. Winter was sure that Mazzic suspected the connection, but never mentioned it, seeing the benefits of the situation.<ref name="Scoundrels" />

Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Solo was contracted by [[Eanjer Kunarazti]] on the planet [[Wukkar/Legends|Wukkar]] to [[Marblewood Estate heist|recover]] 163 million credits stolen from his [[Eanjer Kunarazti's father|father]] by the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] [[Sector Chief]] [[Avrak Villachor]]. Aware that he did not have the skills to pull of the heist alone, Solo enlisted [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] [[Rachele Ree]] to contact a number of individuals to form a specialized team of [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrels]] to pull off the job. Mazzic was one of people that Solo wanted on his team, but he was unavailable. Instead, Mazzic sent his two newest recruits, Winter and Tainer, in his place after reviewing the skills that Solo was looking to acquire for the mission.<ref name="Scoundrels" />

===The Rise of Thrawn===
[[File:Mazzic and Shada.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Mazzic and Shada D'ukal]]
In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="Dating">According to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', ''[[Specter of the Past]]'' is set in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]]. In the novel, it is stated that [[Shada D'ukal]] had been employed by Mazzic for twelve years, giving a date of [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]] for her recruitment.</ref> Mazzic hired [[Shada D'ukal]], a [[Mistryl Shadow Guard]], as his personal [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]].<ref name="SotP">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref> The Mistryl were a secretive group of female warriors<ref name="TLC-SB" /> from the planet [[Emberlene]] who were hired out by their leaders, the [[Eleven Elders of the People]], to support the world after its near destruction at the hands of its enemies. The Eleven assigned D'ukal to work with Mazzic, believing that his organization would grow into a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-spanning network with considerable influence.<ref name="SotP" /> D'ukal refused to discuss her associations with the Mistryl to Mazzic, proclaiming that to do so would break an oath of secrecy. An attractive young woman, she cultivated the image that she was little more that a decoration on Mazzic's arm, and the smuggler allowed rumors to circulate that she was his love interest. D'ukal was, in fact, a highly trained [[Commando/Legends|commando]] with considerable combat prowess.<ref name="TLC-SB" />

In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Mazzic responded to a communication from fellow smuggler [[Samuel Tomas Gillespee]] to meet with him and [[Talon Karrde]] at [[Whistler's Whirlpool Tapcafe]] on the planet [[Trogan/Legends|Trogan]]. Mazzic wasn't the only other smuggler in attendance, being joined by the [[Brubb]] [[Brasck]], [[Par'tah]] the [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]], the [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Ellor]], [[Dravis]], [[Clyngunn]] the [[ZeHethbra]], and [[Niles Ferrier]]. He attended the meeting with D'ukal at his side, and sat with his friend, the [[Gotal/Legends|Gotal]] smuggler [[Lishma]], and listened as Karrde attempted to unite the various smuggler factions against the Empire and their leader, [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], by supplying the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] with intelligence as they gathered it. The Empire had been in retreat since the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] five years previously, but Thrawn's return from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] and his subsequent [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the New Republic was shifting the balance of power in the galaxy. The gathered smugglers were unconvinced; Mazzic felt that the risk of helping the New Republic outweighed the cost of defying the Empire, and chose not to get involved, especially as he heard that Thrawn was paying well for smuggled goods and services, although he admitted having no fondness for Thrawn's newly-grown [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] forces.<ref name="TLC" />

[[File:Ambush on Trogan.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Imperial forces ambush the smuggler meeting on Trogan]]
As the meeting drew to a close with the general consensus being to remain neutral, the assemblage was [[Ambush on Trogan|attacked]] by an [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] contingent from the local [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] under the command of [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Reynol Kosk]]. As the gathered smugglers and their bodyguards fought off the attack, Mazzic concentrated his fire on the [[LAVr QH-7 Chariot|Chariot command speeders]] while D'ukal used all her skills to cut down the enemy. As the smoke cleared, the smugglers had suffered a single casualty—Lishma, who hadn't even been shooting. The Imperial attack and Lishma's death galvanized Mazzic and he agreed to sign on with Karrde. As other members of the group affirmed their own intentions to help and track down Thrawn's clone transfer routes, Mazzic and Ellor decided to strike at the Empire in their own way.<ref name="TLC" />

===Getting Revenge===
[[File:Raid Bilbringi.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Mazzic's raid on Bilbringi destroyed a Star Destroyer under construction]]
Targeting the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]]s at [[Bilbringi system|Bilbringi]], Mazzic led a sabotage team in a daring attack against an under-construction [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] which resulted in the destruction of the half-completed vessel. As the attack group retreated, their exit vector was covered by two [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[DP20 gunship/Legends|gunships]] that Ellor had "borrowed" from the New Republic while they were in dock at refueling stations around [[Duro/Legends|Duro]] that he had part ownership of. Grand Admiral Thrawn, reviewing the incident, used his keen grasp of tactics and strategy to determine that Mazzic had perpetrated the attack with aid from Ellor.<ref name="TLC" />

While investigating the [[Joiol system/Legends|Joiol system]], Mazzic was captured by Imperial forces and taken via [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] to the ''[[Chimaera/Legends|Chimaera]]'' to meet with Grand Admiral Thrawn. Expecting punishment for the destruction of the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], he was surprised when Thrawn told him that he was willing to forget the incident. The Grand Admiral informed him that neither he, nor any senior Imperial official, had ordered the attack on Trogan, and in fact the garrison had strict orders to leave the meeting alone. Mazzic was suspicious of Thrawn's claims until the Grand Admiral pointed out that if he had planned the attack, there would have been no survivors. Mazzic came to believe Thrawn as he had always felt they had got away too easily. Thrawn also informed him that the troops had been hired by a third party to attack the meeting; while Thrawn had his suspicions about who that individual was, he refused to divulge the name. With the meeting concluded, Thrawn arranged for the shuttle to take Mazzic to the backup base on [[Lelmra]] that his organization had retreated to after Mazzic's capture.<ref name="TLC" />

===Confronting Karrde===
Armed with Thrawn's information, Mazzic arranged for a [[Mazzic's slicer friend|friend]] to investigate Imperial military records to confirm Thrawn's claims that Kosk was hired independently. The search turned up proof in Kosk's files that he was hired by Karrde to attack the meeting. Mazzic had suspect that Karrde was the likely culprit since he had the most to gain by uniting the smugglers under his banner. Unknown to Mazzic, Thrawn had planted the evidence in the Imperial records implicating Karrde, and had arranged to have Niles Ferrier use his [[Defel/Legends|Defel]] [[Niles Ferrier's wraith|associate]] to plant matching evidence in Karrde's office aboard the ''[[Wild Karrde]]''.<ref name="TLC" />

[[File:GillespeeMeeting.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Mazzic holds Karrde at blaster-point]]
Believing that Karrde had betrayed them, Mazzic arrived for a meeting on the planet [[Hijarna]] with a squad of enforcers and requested that the meeting take place aboard the ''Wild Karrde''. When Karrde asked why, Mazzic asked him if he had something to hide, to which Karrde replied that he did—as did all of the smugglers assembled. In the end, Karrde relented and took the assembled smuggler chiefs down to the docking bay where the ''Wild Karrde'' was berthed. They were met by [[Dankin]] at the entrance to the ''Wild Karrde'', who sensed that something was amiss, used a coded signal to warn Karrde's people to encircle the group in case of trouble. Mazzic aimed his blaster at Karrde's head to get his people to fall back. Karrde noted that only the eight smuggler chiefs could fit in the ''Wild Karrde''{{'s}} wardroom, to which Mazzic agreed.<ref name="TLC" />

In the wardroom, the eight chiefs—Mazzic, Karrde, Gillespee, Dravis, Ellor, Clyngunn, Par'tah, Brasck and Ferrier—settled around a table. Mazzic requested access to Karrde's office, which Karrde supplied and sent Ellor and Par'tah to retrieve all the [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]]s inside, as well as a couple of [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]]s. While waiting for the pair to return, Mazzic relayed his meeting with Thrawn and his discovery that the attack on Trogan had been a set-up, although he admitted that Karrde hadn't been named specifically but that he was the one with most to gain. Mazzic knew that Imperial records could be falsified, and wanted to check for proof among Karrde's own records that he was the one who hired the Imperial squad. Karrde countered by asking if Mazzic really thought he would leave evidence lying around, and noted that before Mazzic's arrival there had been a suspected intruder aboard the ''Wild Karrde''. Ferrier, who had used his Wraith to plant the evidence Mazzic was seeking, accused Karrde of attempting to deflect the blame.<ref name="TLC" />

[[File:NilesFerrierDeath-TLC4.png|thumb|right|300px|Mazzic's ships destroy Niles Ferrier's gunship]]
Ellor and Par'tah returned, and they started looking through the datacards until they found the planted evidence. During the ensuing conversation, Ferrier slipped up, citing the name of the squad's commanding lieutenant when he shouldn't have known it. Mazzic and Karrde immediately started to suspect something amiss, and started to question Ferrier until Karrde revealed that he had not invited the ship thief to this particular meeting. Mazzic, now coming to suspect Ferrier, turned his blaster on him. Realizing that his cover was blown, Ferrier produced a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]]. While the Wraith held the other chiefs in the wardroom, Ferrier had Karrde take him to the bridge and fly him to his gunship. Mazzic and the others overpowered the Wraith, but could not stop Ferrier from reaching his ship and taking off, trapping the ''Wild Karrde'' under a [[conner net]]. As the gunship ascended into orbit, Karrde allowed Mazzic to call in the ''Raptor'' and ''Skyclaw'' to destroy the gunship.<ref name="TLC" />

===The Battle of Bilbringi===
Mazzic met with Karrde and Gillespee in a tapcafe, where Karrde passed on information to them that the New Republic was looking to secure a [[Crystal gravfield trap|CGT array]]. Karrde's information indicated that the New Republic was committing two whole [[Sector fleet/Legends|sector fleets]] to engage the Empire at [[Tangrene system/Legends|Tangrene]] in an effort to secure one. Karrde was of the opinion that if the New Republic was willing to fight for one, they would be equally willing to buy one off of them. When Mazzic asked if Karrde intended to sneak into Tangrene ahead of the New Republic and nab the array, he was surprised when Karrde instead intended to target the CGT array at Bilbringi.<ref name="TLC" />

Mazzic thought Karrde was crazy since half the Imperial fleet was stationed there. Karrde countered that Thrawn would probably only leave a skeleton force there since he would anticipate the New Republic's move at Tangrene. Mazzic considered the logic of the move, pointing out that the security procedures had probably been overhauled since Mazzic and Ellor had raided the shipyards. Karrde considered that to be a bonus in their favor since the Empire would not have found the holes in their new security setup yet. Mazzic provisionally agreed to the mission as long as Karrde could get confirmation on the timing of the Tangrene assault since he did not want to be at Bilbringi if there was a chance that Thrawn was close by.<ref name="TLC" />

[[File:Bilbringi.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Mazzic and the other smugglers are caught up in the Battle of Bilbringi]]
Mazzic brought his forces to Bilbringi, stealthily inserting themselves into the usual [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] traffic. Mazzic's part of the plan was to go for the CGT array at the main command center. As they waited, an Imperial fleet exited [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], and Mazzic, along with several of the other smugglers committed to the raid, believed that Karrde had sold them out. Mazzic intended to go down fighting, and [[Aves]] offered him command of the combined smuggler forces. Mazzic intended to take it anyway, and told the smugglers they had to deliver their first punch together. However, Aves realized that the Imperial fleet was not configured in an enclosure formation but for entrapment, coming to the conclusion that Imperials were not there for the smugglers. The New Republic assault on Tangrene was actually [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|targeting]] Bilbringi. Mazzic told the assembled smugglers not to panic; the New Republic's presence would distract the Empire so they should stay on schedule and see what happened.<ref name="TLC" />

After the arrival of the New Republic fleet, Mazzic and the smugglers prepared to grab the CGT array and make a run for it. Aves contacted [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], to inform him of the situation and their plan. To the smugglers, it did not matter what direction they went after securing the array, but it might matter to the New Republic. Aves and Antilles agreed on a direction for the smugglers to take on their way out, hitting a [[Golan I Space Defense Platform/Legends|Golan I]] station from behind as the New Republic attacked from the front. As the smugglers punched through, it opened a hole in the Imperial defenses that the New Republic could exploit. The Battle of Bilbringi ended with an Imperial retreat after Thrawn was assassinated by his own bodyguard, [[Rukh/Legends|Rukh]].<ref name="TLC" />

===The Caamas Document Crisis===
In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]],<ref name="ERC" /> Talon Karrde sent word to Mazzic and Clyngunn's people informing them to apprehend the [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Lak Jit]] if he approached them. Jit had uncovered a datacard from the [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|Mount Tantiss]] ruins on [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]] revealing that the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] were complicit in the [[Caamas Firestorm|devastation]] of the planet [[Caamas/Legends|Caamas]]. Mazzic was contacted by a [[Garoos]] associate, [[Cromf]], who had found Jit. A meeting was arranged, and the smuggler agreed to meet the Devaronian at the [[Dona Laza]] [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcafe]]. Mazzic took D'ukal along with him, with [[Griv]] as backup. Despite Karrde's insistence that Jit not be allowed to talk to anyone, Mazzic wanted to hear what he had to say. Due to having to pay Cromf a finder's fee for Jit, Mazzic was annoyed he was not going to break even on this venture and wanted to know if the information that Jit was carrying was worth anything. If so, then Mazzic was determined to get a cut of it. D'ukal visibly showed her disdain for Mazzic's plan, causing him to remind her that was how the game was played.<ref name="SotP" />

Jit arrived at the tapcafe, and Mazzic greeted him with a glass of [[Vistulo brandale]]. Jit began by telling Mazzic that the information he had could not be verified since he no longer had the evidence, and that he knew he could not ask for money in exchange. Mazzic made a show of promising payment if the value of the information was worth it. Jit relayed the information that not only was [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] responsible for the destruction of Caamas, but that the [[planetary shield generator]]s had been sabotaged by a group of Bothans. The theory that Palpatine had been behind the assault was a long-held belief, but the information implicating the Bothans was new. After hearing Jit's story, Mazzic had D'ukal render the Devaronian unconscious with a swift blow to the base of his left horn. Taking Jit into custody for Karrde, Mazzic wondered if he could bargain Karrde's bounty up. When D'ukal asked him what they were going to do with the information, Mazzic informed her that no one was going to care about a planet destroyed half a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] ago.<ref name="SotP" />

Shortly after handing Jit over to Karrde, Mazzic travelled to [[Borcorash]] with D'ukal and Griv. A mutually profitable trade had been arranged with a [[Kubaz/Legends|Kubaz]] representing a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|cartel]]. In exchange for a small case of [[Sormahil fire gem]]s, Mazzic agreed to deliver a case of [[Ryll/Legends|ryll]]. Meeting at the ultra-private top floor of the [[Resinem Entertainment Complex]], Mazzic had his suspicions about the meeting. While Griv accompanied him to the meeting, D'ukal was tasked with scaling the complex and covering them from above. The exchange occurred without incident, with both sides checking the merchandise they received. As Mazzic bid farewell to the Kubaz and his Human associate, he heard a tapping on the room's skylight. Above, D'ukal had a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] trained on the Kubaz, having appropriated it from an [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] who the Kubaz had set up to kill Mazzic. Mazzic threw D'ukal a little salute, leaving the room with Griv. Shortly after, D'ukal resigned as his bodyguard.<ref name="SotP" />

==Personality and traits==
Mazzic was a tall, muscular, Human male with long shoulder-length [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. He was considered to be a good smuggler and an excellent pilot, and the people in his organization respected him as a leader.<ref name="TLC-SB" /> Mazzic projected an air of a suave, sophisticated ladies man<ref name="TLC" /> and was known to have good taste.<ref name="VotF">''[[Vision of the Future]]''</ref> However, Mazzic had a suspicious nature and was a poor strategist, distrusting anyone who was not part of his organization.<ref name="TLC-SB" />

No-nonsense and forthright, Mazzic had little tolerance for deception<ref name="TLC-SB" /> but was fanatical about preserving his own secrets. He had many friends within the smuggling community, and while he did not put his trust in many people, those who he did come to trust he treated as if they were lifelong friends.<ref name="Gamer 3" /> However, Mazzic had those individuals thoroughly checked out first before placing his trust in them. Others considered Mazzic to be trustworthy and competent, and a good judge of character. People such as Han Solo were willing to trust anyone Mazzic vouched for, although they did admit that he had been proven wrong in the past.<ref name="Scoundrels" />

Prior to Grand Admiral Thrawn's campaign against the New Republic, Mazzic was willing to take the money the Empire offered while still be suspicious of their motives.<ref name="TLC-SB" /> He was also willing to deal with the New Republic depending on who was offering the most money.<ref name="TLC" /> That changed following the Imperial attack on the smuggler meeting at the Whistler's Whirlpool Tapcafe; Mazzic became reckless after the death of his friend Lishma,<ref name="TLC-SB" /> and his keen sense of justice<ref name="Gamer 3" /> made him determined to make Empire pay for coming after him and his friends.<ref name="TLC-SB" />

==Skills and abilities==
Mazzic was a trained combatant, physically fit and able to engage in both blaster and [[Melee/Legends|melee]] combat. He was knowledgeable about [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], [[Language/Legends|languages]], and planetary [[Star system/Legends|star systems]], and was particularly well versed in the [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]].<ref name="TLC-SB" /> Mazzic was fluent in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] and [[Mistryl battle language|Mistryl]].<ref name="Gamer 3" />

As a smuggler, Mazzic was streetwise and capable of bargaining, conning, or persuading his way to obtaining items of value. He was also a competent [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] and [[Forger (profession)/Legends|forger]]. An excellent [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], Mazzic was comfortable flying space [[Transport/Legends|transports]], plotting [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogation]] routes, and operating the craft's sensors, weapons, and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]]. He was capable of repairing starship systems, as well as [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]]. Mazzic also had first aid training.<ref name="TLC-SB" />

==Equipment==
Mazzic carried a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]] and a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. He typically wore a black [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] accompanied by a short black [[Cloak/Legends|cloak]]. As the head of a smuggling operation, he owned a ''Starwind''-class pleasure yacht, the ''Distant Rainbow'', and a pair of ''Preybird''-class starfighters, the ''Raptor'' and the ''Skyclaw''.<ref name="TLC-SB" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Mazzic-TLC4.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Mazzic depicted as a Myke in ''The Last Command'' comic adaptation]]
Mazzic first appeared in the [[1993]] novel ''[[The Last Command]]'' by [[Timothy Zahn]].<ref name="TLC" /> The character was identified as a Human in [[West End Games]]' ''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'', published a year later in [[1994]].<ref name="TLC-SB" /> In [[1998]], [[Dark Horse Comics]] published a comic book adaptation of ''[[Star Wars: The Last Command (comic series)| The Last Command]]''; in that series, Mazzic was illustrated as a dark-haired, blue-skinned alien<ref name="TLC2">''[[The Last Command 2]]''</ref> later identified as a [[Myke]], a [[Near-Human]] species, in ''[[Star Wars Handbook 2: Crimson Empire]]''.<ref name="HB2">''[[Star Wars Handbook 2: Crimson Empire]]''</ref> However, later sources, such as the article "[[The Smugglers Alliance]]" in ''[[Star Wars Gamer 3]]'', continued to identify Mazzic as a Human.<ref name="Gamer 3" /> As the majority of sources dealing with Mazzic's species identify him as a Human, this article assumes that Mazzic is a Human from Corellia and not a Myke as depicted in ''[[Star Wars: The Last Command (comic series)|The Last Command]]'' comic series.
